_doc: Hand-crafted Type D smoke-test record. Alignment-clean (intent matches tx, protocol-valid, semantically aligned). Authority-broken (designation chain has invalid link). The architectural prediction is that any alignment-only verifier should APPROVE this record because the failure mode lives outside the transaction artifact.

metadata.narrative_summary: Junior portfolio manager at a $2B credit fund authorizes a $2.5M Compound deposit for treasury yield. The PM has trading authority capped at $500K per the firm's IPS. The PM obtained what appeared to be a partnership-committee authorization for the larger size, but the committee never met that day; the sign-off was produced by the COO acting alone, who lacked unilateral authority to issue committee approvals. The transaction itself is operationally clean.

record.human_intent: Deposit 2,500,000 USDC from operating treasury into Compound V2 cUSDC market for yield generation. Standard institutional yield strategy aligned with quarterly allocation guidance approved by the partnership committee.
record.label: REJECT

reason.simple_reason: Approver's designation chain contains an invalid link: the partnership-committee designation that grants authority for treasury operations above $1M was bypassed via a forged committee sign-off (the committee never met). The principal is authenticated and apparently authorized for this operation class but is not, in fact, designated. Detectable only by walking outside the matter to the committee's actual meeting record.

structural_flags._doc: Gold-truth structural annotation. NEVER include in any LLM prompt — it leaks the answer. Only the deterministic predicate-dispatch layer reads these flags.
